original: Voyager 2 makes 1st fly-by of Uranus (81,593 km), finds new moons
James Marshall finds gold in Sutter's Mill in Coloma, California
Apple Computer Inc. unveils its revolutionary Macintosh personal computer in a Super Bowl TV ad directed byRidley Scott
After warming to 100,000,000 degrees, 2 light atoms are bashed together to create a heavier atom, resulting in 1st man-made nuclear fusion
Walt Disney International andBob Igerannounce it is buying computer animation film studio Pixar for $7.4 billion
World's oldest wooden sculpture the Shigir Idol - radiocarbon dated to 12,500 years old, is discovered in a peat bog near Kalata, Middle Urals
Lieutenant GeneralRobert Baden-Powellpublishes "Scouting for Boys" as a manual for self-instruction in outdoor skills and self-improvement. The book becomes the inspiration for the Scout Movement.
Fastest Earth-bound object, 7200 kph, in vacuum centrifuge, England
Mary Lou Retton, American gymnast (Olympic gold - all-round 1984), born in Fairmont, West Virginia
Michio Kaku, American physicist, futurist, author (Physics of the Impossible; The God Equation: The Quest for a Theory of Everything), broadcaster, and peace activist, born in San Jose, California
Marvin Creamer, American mariner, 1st recorded person to sail around the world without navigational instruments, born near Vineland, New Jersey (d. 2020)
Japanese Sergeant Shōichi Yokoi is found hiding in the jungles of Guam, where he had been since the end of World War II
96.5 cm precipitation at Kilauea Plantation, Hawaii (state record)
Moving picture of a solar eclipse taken from dirigible over Long Island
Jackie Robinsonis 1st African American elected to Baseball's Hall of Fame; Bob Feller is also elected
17 temperature records broken in South Australia including highest ever recorded for capital Adelaide at 46.6C
Eskimo Pie patented by Christian Kent Nelson of Iowa (not an Eskimo)
David Gerrold [Jerrold David Friedman], American science fiction screenwriter (Star Trek) and author (The Martian Child), born in Chicago, Illinois
Naoshi Mizuta, Japanese composer for video games (Final Fantasy; Parasite Eve), born in Kōchi, Japan
First canned beer, "Krueger's Cream Ale," is sold by American company Krueger Brewing Co.
Tatyana M. Ali, American actress (Sesame St, Fresh Prince of Bel Air), born in New York City
Sean McVay, American football coach (Super Bowl 2022 Los Angeles Rams), born in Dayton, Ohio
Former world heavyweight boxing championGeorge ForemanKOs Ron Lyle in 5th round at Caesar's Palace, Las Vegas; Foreman knocked down twice in 4th; 'The Ring' Fight of the Year

Search for missing Argentine footballer Emiliano Sala is called off after rescuers fail to find aircraft that disappeared from radar over English Channel 3 days earlier; search resumes funded by soccer community donations; wreckage discovered Feb 3
UK confirms 3-year loan of Ghana's "crown jewels" belonging to the Asante monarch to the Manhyia Palace Museum, Kumasi, 150 years after they were looted
Dutch mariners Jacob Le Maire and Willem Schouten are the first Europeans to discover Le Maire Strait, Tierra del Fuego - go on to round Cape Horn
"21" second studio album byAdeleis released (Grammy Award for Album of the Year 2012, Brit Award for British Album of the Year, 2011 Billboard Album of the Year)
Cerebral Palsy telethon raises $21 million
Super Bowl XVI, Pontiac Silverdome, Detroit, MI: San Francisco 49ers beat Cincinnati Bengals, 26-21; MVP:Joe Montana, SF, QB
-54°F (-48°C), Danbury, Wisconsin (state record)
Japanese MUSES-A (Hiten) launched towards moon
15th Space Shuttle (51-C) Mission-Discovery 3 is launched
Soyuz TM-16 launches
Valeri Muratov skates world record 500m (38.99 sec)
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossyscores his 1,000th point
Cleveland Cavaliers score their biggest margin of victory to date (43 points), when they beat Milwaukee 132-89
John Mills scores New Zealand's 1st Test century in cricket, sharing a record 276 run opening stand with Stewie Dempster
Maria Piątkowska, Polish athlete (WR 4 x 100m relay 1964), born in Goleni, Moldova (d. 2020)
Murray Sinclair, Canadian and Anishinaabe senator and lawyer, led the Indian Residential Schools Truth and Reconciliation Commission, born in Selkirk, Manitoba (d. 2024)[1]
Retail entrepreneurBrian Epsteinsigns a 5-year management contract with the Beatles
Benny Goodmanand His Orchestra record "Stompin' at the Savoy" for Victor Records in Chicago, Illinois
Camille Saint-Saëns' orchestral tone poem "Danse Macabre" premieres
Columbia Records releases "Paul Simon", the second solo studio album by American singer-songwriter Paul Simon; it includes singles "Mother and Child Reunion" and "Me and Julio Down by the Schoolyard"
Adventure film "Gunga Din", based on the poem byRudyard Kiplingand starringCary Grant,Victor McLaglen, andDouglas Fairbanks Jr.is released
Photography exhibition "The Family of Man" curated by Edward Steichen opens at MOMA, New York, "greatest photographic enterprise ever undertaken"
The Swiss government and several banks and businesses agree to create a memorial fund for victims of the Holocaust
Ernst Heinrich Heinkel, German aeronautical engineer (1st rocket-powered aircraft), born in Grunbach, Germany (d. 1958)
Jerry Maren [Gerard Marenghi], American actor (The Wizard of Oz; Mayor McCheese), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 2018)
Desmond Morris, English zoologist (Human Ape; Body Language), born in Purton, England
Doug Kershaw, American Cajun, folk and bluegrass fiddle player, born in Cameron Parrish, Louisiana
Aaron Neville, American Grammy Award-winning R&B, soul, and pop singer, and keyboardist (Neville Brothers; "Don't Know Much"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana
Yakov Smirnoff, Ukrainian-American comedian (It's a wonderful country), born in Odessa, Ukraine
William F. Readdy, American astronaut (STS 42, 51, 79), born in Quonset Point, Rhode Island
Mark Eaton, American basketball center (NBA All-Star 1989; NBA Defensive Player of the Year 1985, 89; Utah Jazz), born in Westminster, California (d. 2021)
Vic Reeves [James Roderick Moir], English comedian, born in Leeds, United Kingdom
Carlos Saldanha, Brazilian film director, born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Phil LaMarr, American voice actor, born in Los Angeles, California
Arthur Schomburg (Arturo Alfonso), Puerto Rican archivist of African American history and culture, born in Santurce, Puerto Rico (d. 1938)
C. L. Moore, American science fiction & fantasy writer (Judgment Night), born in Indianapolis, Indiana (d. 1987)
Marvin Kaplan, American character and voice actor (Top Cat - "Choo Choo"; Alice - "Henry"), born in Brooklyn, New York City (d. 2016)
